project.name: kaiord
project.description: Kaiord is an open-source health & fitness data framework: a TypeScript monorepo for creating, converting, and managing workout/activity data across FIT, TCX, ZWO, GCN, and KRD formats. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: discoverFiles

nodes.name: isHardcodedExcluded
nodes.summary: Determines whether a file path should be unconditionally excluded from scanning based on hardcoded rules for dependency directories, build artifacts, binary extensions, and generated files.

nodes.name: compileGlob
nodes.summary: Compiles a gitignore-style glob pattern into a pair of regular expressions (one for directory matching, one for file matching) for efficient path filtering.

nodes.name: parseIgnore
nodes.summary: Parses a gitignore-format ignore file content into an array of compiled ignore rules, handling negation prefixes, trailing-slash directory markers, and comment stripping.

nodes.name: makeIgnoreMatcher
nodes.summary: Creates a matcher function from compiled ignore rules that, given a file path, returns whether it should be ignored accounting for both directory and file pattern rules.

nodes.name: detectLanguage
nodes.summary: Detects the programming language of a file based on its extension and special filename patterns.

nodes.name: detectCategory
nodes.summary: Categorizes a file into one of the known semantic categories (code, config, docs, infra, data, script, markup) based on path segments, filename, and extension heuristics.

nodes.name: countLines
nodes.summary: Counts the total number of lines in a batch of files using the system wc utility with a fallback to direct file reads for reliability.

nodes.name: parseWc
nodes.summary: Parses the output of the wc -l command into a map from file path to line count.

nodes.name: detectFrameworks

nodes.name: detectName
nodes.summary: Infers the project name from package.json, Cargo.toml, go.mod, or pyproject.toml, falling back to the directory basename.

nodes.name: loadTsAliases
nodes.summary: Loads TypeScript path alias mappings from all tsconfig files in the project, normalizing them to relative path prefixes for import resolution.

nodes.name: resolveAlias
nodes.summary: Resolves a TypeScript path alias import specifier to a concrete project-relative file path using precomputed alias sets.

nodes.name: extractTsImports
nodes.summary: Extracts all import specifier strings from TypeScript/JavaScript file content using regex patterns for static imports, side-effect imports, require calls, and dynamic imports.

nodes.name: resolveTsImports
nodes.summary: Resolves all imports in a TypeScript/JavaScript file to project-relative paths, handling relative paths, path aliases, and index file probing.

nodes.name: resolvePyImports
nodes.summary: Resolves all imports in a Python file to project-relative paths, handling `from x import y`, `import x`, and relative import syntax with package directory probing.

nodes.name: tryPyModule
nodes.summary: Attempts to resolve a Python module path to either a .py file or a package __init__.py within the project file set.

nodes.name: buildImportMap
nodes.summary: Builds a complete import map for all project files by dispatching to language-specific resolvers (TypeScript, Python, shell) and returning a record of file-to-imports arrays.

nodes.languageNotes: Uses a jittered exponential backoff: delay = min(baseDelay * 2^attempt * random, maxDelay), tested via an injectable randomFn parameter for determinism.

nodes.name: cascade-zones.ts
nodes.summary: Implements boundary cascading logic when a zone's min or max is edited: fixes same-zone inversions and propagates forward/backward to keep neighboring zone boundaries consistent.
nodes.languageNotes: Four cooperating helper functions implement a cascade algorithm: fixSameZone corrects intra-zone inversion, cascadeForward/cascadeBackward propagate min/max equality constraints to adjacent zones.

nodes.name: cascadeToNeighbors
nodes.summary: Entry point for cascade logic: applies intra-zone fix then propagates boundaries in both directions from the edited index.

nodes.name: fixSameZone
nodes.summary: Corrects an intra-zone inversion where min exceeds max by clamping the opposite boundary to match.

nodes.name: cascadeForward
nodes.summary: Propagates min boundary changes forward through subsequent zones so each zone's min equals the previous zone's max.

nodes.name: cascadeBackward
nodes.summary: Propagates max boundary changes backward through preceding zones, clamping to zero minimum.

nodes.name: useCoachingActivities
nodes.summary: Aggregates coaching activities from all linked coaching sources for the active profile, providing filtered activity lists and sync/connect callbacks.

nodes.name: use-coaching-auto-sync-helpers.ts
nodes.summary: Provides pure helper functions for coaching auto-sync: staleness detection and the core async sync runner that interacts with persistence and analytics.

nodes.name: runSourceSync
nodes.summary: Executes a coaching source sync if the last sync is stale, recording analytics events for skip, success, and failure cases.

nodes.name: use-coaching-auto-sync.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that automatically synchronizes all linked coaching sources for the active profile when the component mounts or week changes, running syncs sequentially via an async effect.

nodes.name: useCoachingAutoSync
nodes.summary: Runs an auto-sync effect for all linked coaching sources when syncSources or weekStart change, filtering sources to only those linked to the active profile.

nodes.name: use-discovered-bridges.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that subscribes to the bridge discovery store and returns a reactive list of all currently discovered browser extension bridges.

nodes.name: use-discovered-extension-id.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that subscribes to bridge discovery and returns the discovered extension ID for a given bridge ID, using useSyncExternalStore for consistency.

nodes.name: use-dismiss-auto-match-banner.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that provides a callback to persistently dismiss the auto-match coaching banner by writing a dismissal timestamp via the persistence context.

nodes.name: use-dnd-card-wrapper.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that wraps dnd-kit's useSortable to provide drag-and-drop card behavior including style transforms and attribute bindings for sortable workout cards.

nodes.name: useDndCardWrapper
nodes.summary: Integrates dnd-kit sortable logic for a card element, returning transform style, listeners, attributes, and ref for drag-and-drop functionality.

nodes.name: use-editor-context-menu.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that builds the context menu handler for the workout editor, computing available actions (copy, paste, delete) based on the current selection and clipboard state.

nodes.name: use-executed-match-auto.ts
nodes.summary: React hooks that automatically append executed workout matches to session match records, with calendar-level and flat-list variants, using microtask deferral to avoid render-phase writes.

nodes.name: useExecutedMatchAuto
nodes.summary: Watches for session matches missing executed workout IDs and appends matching workouts via an effect, tracking already-fired match IDs to prevent duplicate writes.

nodes.name: use-focus-on-route-change.ts
nodes.summary: Accessibility hook that moves keyboard focus to the main content area on every SPA route change, using MutationObserver and rAF with a fallback timeout to handle async DOM rendering.

nodes.name: useFocusOnRouteChange
nodes.summary: Triggers focus on a data-focus-target element after each route change, using MutationObserver to wait for async rendering and a rAF+timeout fallback strategy.

nodes.name: use-garmin-bridge-action-helpers.ts
nodes.summary: Stateless helper functions for Garmin bridge actions: looking up the extension ID, detecting the extension via ping, and pushing workout data through the bridge transport.

nodes.name: runPush
nodes.summary: Executes a GCN workout push to the Garmin bridge extension, managing push/redetect state and handling success and failure paths.

nodes.name: use-garmin-bridge-actions.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that exposes stateful Garmin bridge action callbacks (detect, push, list) along with UI state flags (pushing, session active, errors) for components interacting with the Garmin bridge extension.

nodes.name: useGarminBridgeActions
nodes.summary: Composes bridge action helpers and UI state into a single hook that provides detect, push, and list callbacks for the Garmin bridge integration.

nodes.name: use-garmin-detection.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that triggers an initial Garmin extension detection on mount when a discovered extension ID is available.

nodes.name: use-latest-ref.ts
nodes.summary: Generic React utility hook that keeps a ref synchronized to the latest value of a variable, useful for avoiding stale closures in effects.

nodes.name: use-lazy-dialog.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that manages lazy dialog mounting state, deferring DOM rendering until the dialog is first opened to avoid unnecessary layout cost.

nodes.name: use-library-templates-live.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that returns a live Dexie query over all workout templates in the library, auto-updating when the templates table changes.

nodes.name: use-match-session.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that provides a callback to create a new session match record linking a calendar activity to a workout, persisted via a Dexie transaction.

nodes.name: use-matched-sessions-heal.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that repairs dangling session match records with malformed coaching activity ID shapes by running a heal operation via microtask, tracking already-healed IDs to prevent duplicate runs.

nodes.name: use-matched-sessions-hydrate-helpers.ts
nodes.summary: Pure helper functions for session hydration: collecting all referenced workout IDs from a set of matches and resolving executed workout records from a lookup map.

nodes.name: use-matched-sessions-hydrate.ts
nodes.summary: Async function that hydrates raw session match records by fetching associated coaching activities and workouts from Dexie, computing compliance scores and separating matched from dangling records.

nodes.name: hydrateMatchedSessions
nodes.summary: Enriches raw SessionMatch records with coaching activity data, compliance scores, and resolved executed workouts, separating fully matched from dangling records.

nodes.name: use-matched-sessions-perf.ts
nodes.summary: Performance instrumentation helpers for useMatchedSessions, using the Web Performance API to mark and measure query duration via named performance marks.

nodes.name: use-matched-sessions-query.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a Dexie query function that fetches session match records for a specific profile within a week's date range using a compound index.

nodes.name: use-matched-sessions.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that orchestrates the full matched sessions pipeline: live Dexie query, hydration with coaching data, performance instrumentation, and session ID shape healing.

nodes.name: useMatchedSessions
nodes.summary: Combines Dexie live query, hydration, perf marks, and ID-shape healing to return hydrated matched session data for a profile's week.

nodes.name: use-onboarding-tutorial.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that determines whether to show the onboarding tutorial, suppressing it in E2E test environments and when the user has already completed onboarding.

nodes.name: use-pickable-workouts.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that returns a live list of workouts pickable for a session match on a given date and sport, filtering out workouts already matched and normalizing sport family for comparison.

nodes.name: usePickableWorkouts
nodes.summary: Fetches workouts for a date and sport family, excludes those already matched to other sessions, and returns the filtered list via useLiveQuery.

nodes.name: use-profile-by-id-live.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that returns a live Dexie query for a single profile by ID, resolving to undefined when the ID is absent.

nodes.name: use-profile-snapshot-push-helpers.ts
nodes.summary: Stateless helpers for profile snapshot bridge delivery: sport selection, queued snapshot push, and queued snapshot clear operations over the shared bridge operation queue.

nodes.name: sendSnapshot
nodes.summary: Enqueues a profile-snapshot bridge message to a specific extension, updating the fingerprint map on success.

nodes.name: sendClear
nodes.summary: Enqueues a profile-snapshot-clear bridge message, removing the fingerprint map entry on success.

nodes.name: use-profile-snapshot-push.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that reactively pushes the active profile snapshot to all discovered bridges when the profile or bridges change, skipping unchanged fingerprints and clearing stale ones.

nodes.name: useProfileSnapshotPush
nodes.summary: Synchronizes the active profile snapshot to all discovered bridges on profile or bridge changes, using fingerprinting to skip redundant pushes and clearing removed bridges.

nodes.name: use-profiles-live.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that returns a live Dexie query over all profiles, auto-updating whenever the profiles table changes.

nodes.name: use-route-announcer-label.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that derives a human-readable screen-reader announcement label from the current route pathname for accessibility route announcers.

nodes.name: use-set-calendar-view.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that exposes a memoized callback to persist the user's chosen calendar view (e.g., week/month) to Dexie via the setCalendarView application use case.

nodes.name: use-set-user-preference-fields.ts
nodes.summary: React hook providing a memoized callback to update arbitrary user preference fields in Dexie, delegating to the setUserPreferenceFields application use case.

nodes.name: use-storage-probe.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal React hook that triggers a storage availability probe via the storage Zustand store on mount.

nodes.name: use-store-hydration.ts
nodes.summary: Composite React hook that bootstraps all external-service detection (storage probe, Garmin, Train2Go, bridge discovery) as part of app initialization.

nodes.name: use-train2go-detection.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that detects the Train2Go Chrome extension on mount and on page visibility change, updating the train2go Zustand store accordingly.

nodes.name: use-train2go-supports-zones.ts
nodes.summary: React hook using useSyncExternalStore to reactively expose whether the Train2Go bridge supports training zones based on bridge capabilities.

nodes.name: use-unmatch-session.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that provides a memoized callback to unlink a coaching session match via the unmatchSession use case within a Dexie transaction.

nodes.name: use-user-preferences.ts
nodes.summary: React hook using Dexie's useLiveQuery to reactively read user preferences for a given profile, synthesizing a default record when none exists.

nodes.name: use-v10-migration-toast.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that displays a toast notification once after a Dexie v10 database migration completes, also firing an analytics event for the migration outcome.

nodes.name: useAppHandlers.ts
nodes.summary: React hook aggregating top-level app event handlers for file loading, step selection, and workout creation, wiring store actions to DOM-level callbacks.

nodes.name: useDeleteCleanup.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that sets up a recurring interval (via setInterval) to purge expired soft-delete records from the workout store.

nodes.name: useKeyboardShortcuts.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that registers global keydown and keyup listeners on window, delegating to createKeyDownHandler and createEscapeHandler factories built from the provided handler callbacks.

nodes.name: useToast.helpers.ts
nodes.summary: Pure helper functions for the toast system: creates variant-specific toast callables, generates random toast IDs, and assembles ToastItem objects.

nodes.name: useToast.ts
nodes.summary: Core React hook managing toast notification state with add, dismiss, mark-visible, and per-variant (info/success/error/warn) convenience methods.

nodes.name: useToast.types.ts
nodes.summary: Type definitions for the toast notification system, re-exporting or extending types from the Toast atom component.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Root CSS file for the workout SPA editor, defining global Tailwind base layers, CSS custom properties (design tokens), and application-wide style resets.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: AI agent guidance document for the src/lib/ directory, cataloguing key files by category (runtime/config, AI, zone math, crypto/storage, routing, sport canonicalization, analytics PII, React utils, generated/static) with working instructions and dependency notes.

nodes.name: ai-sdk-gateway-stub.ts
nodes.summary: Stub/adapter that creates and exports a singleton AI SDK gateway instance alongside a GatewayAuthenticationError class for distinguishing auth failures in the AI provider pipeline.

nodes.name: GatewayAuthenticationError
nodes.summary: Custom error class for AI gateway authentication failures, with a static isInstance type guard for narrowing caught errors.

nodes.name: build-route-error-payload.ts
nodes.summary: Utility function that constructs a sanitized route error payload by scrubbing PII from the error message, stack, component stack, and URL before analytics or logging.

nodes.name: canonicalize-sport.ts
nodes.summary: Pure utility that maps freeform sport name strings to canonical KRD sport enum values using a lookup table with normalized string matching.

nodes.name: crypto.ts
nodes.summary: Browser-native AES-GCM encrypt/decrypt utilities using the Web Crypto API with PBKDF2 key derivation, producing Base64-encoded ciphertext for secure local storage.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the browser's SubtleCrypto API (AES-GCM + PBKDF2) with no external dependencies; salt and IV are prepended to ciphertext for self-contained storage.

nodes.name: encrypt
nodes.summary: Encrypts a plaintext string using AES-GCM with PBKDF2-derived key, returning a Base64 blob containing salt + IV + ciphertext.

nodes.name: decrypt
nodes.summary: Decrypts a Base64-encoded AES-GCM blob produced by encrypt, extracting salt/IV and returning the original plaintext string.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: AI agent guidance for the fitsdk-minimal subdirectory, explaining that profile.js is a large generated file from the Garmin FIT SDK that should never be hand-edited.

nodes.name: profile.js
nodes.summary: Auto-generated Garmin FIT SDK profile data file (~9700 lines) containing all FIT message types, field definitions, and enum values used by the minimal FIT decoder in the SPA editor.
nodes.languageNotes: This is a large generated JavaScript data file; it should never be manually edited — regenerate from the official Garmin FIT SDK profile.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: AI agent guidance for the focus/ subdirectory, describing the overlay-count and focus-fallback chain utilities that manage keyboard focus when Radix UI overlays are open.

nodes.name: fallback-chain.ts
nodes.summary: Pure function that resolves which DOM element should receive keyboard focus using a priority fallback chain: specific target item, first item, empty-state button, then editor heading.

nodes.name: resolveFocusElement
nodes.summary: Walks a priority fallback chain to find the best focusable element given a desired focus target, registered items, and fallback DOM refs.

nodes.name: overlay-count.ts
nodes.summary: Utility that counts open Radix UI overlay elements in a given DOM root by detecting elements with Radix data attributes.

nodes.name: overlay-mutation-observer.ts
nodes.summary: MutationObserver-based overlay count watcher that notifies subscribers when the overlay count changes, with a degraded polling fallback when MutationObserver is unavailable.

nodes.name: overlay-observer.ts
nodes.summary: Manages a per-root registry of overlay count subscriptions, exposing subscribeToOverlayCount for consumers and a test-only reset helper.

nodes.name: overlay-singleton.ts
nodes.summary: Singleton state holder for the overlay observer registry, using a global window property in production and a module-level variable in test environments to avoid cross-iframe state leakage.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a window-global variable in production to survive React hot-reloads, but falls back to a module-level variable during tests to prevent cross-test contamination.

nodes.name: generate-workout.ts
nodes.summary: AI workout generation entry point that uses the configured language model provider to convert a natural-language prompt into a KRD workout object.

nodes.name: generateWorkoutKrd
nodes.summary: Orchestrates AI workout generation: creates a language model, runs text-to-workout conversion, and assembles the result into a KRD workout.

nodes.name: hr-methods.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the HR_METHODS configuration array listing all supported heart rate zone calculation methods (e.g. % max HR, LTHR, HRR) used to drive zone-method selectors in the SPA editor.

nodes.name: merge-refs.ts
nodes.summary: Exports a mergeRefs utility that combines multiple React ref objects or callbacks into a single ref, enabling components to attach more than one ref to the same DOM node.

nodes.name: mergeRefs
nodes.summary: Merges an arbitrary number of React ref callbacks/objects into a single ref handler.

nodes.name: pace-methods.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the PACE_METHODS configuration array listing supported pace zone calculation methods for running workouts in the SPA editor.

nodes.name: power-methods-advanced.ts
nodes.summary: Provides COGGAN_7 and FRIEL_7 power zone system definitions using a builder helper, representing the two dominant 7-zone cycling power training frameworks.

nodes.name: power-methods.ts
nodes.summary: Exports POWER_METHODS, the complete list of power zone calculation method definitions assembled from basic and advanced zone systems, used to populate zone-method selectors in the SPA editor.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: AI agent guidance for the profile-snapshot directory, documenting the purpose of profileToSnapshot conversion, key files, and internal dependencies.

nodes.name: profile-to-snapshot.ts
nodes.summary: Converts a user Profile record into a compact ProfileSnapshot used by the workout editor, mapping sport-specific thresholds (cycling FTP, running lactate threshold, swimming CSS) and heart rate data into a snapshot that can be embedded in workout generation context.

nodes.name: profileToSnapshot
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full conversion from a user Profile and active sport into a ProfileSnapshot, calling sport-specific threshold and HR builders.

nodes.name: buildHeartRate
nodes.summary: Builds the heart rate section of a ProfileSnapshot from a user profile and active sport.

nodes.name: provider-factory.ts
nodes.summary: Factory function createLanguageModel that dynamically imports and instantiates an AI SDK provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, or Google Generative AI) based on the stored AI provider configuration.

nodes.name: createLanguageModel
nodes.summary: Dynamically imports and returns the appropriate AI language model instance (Anthropic/OpenAI/Google) based on runtime provider configuration.

nodes.name: provider-models.ts
nodes.summary: Defines PROVIDER_MODELS, the static map of AI provider identifiers to their available model IDs, and getDefaultModel, a utility to retrieve the default model for a given provider.

nodes.name: raw-hash.ts
nodes.summary: Computes a deterministic SHA-256 hex hash of a raw workout fragment (title, description, comments) using the Web Crypto API, enabling stable change detection for calendar fragments.

nodes.name: computeRawHash
nodes.summary: Computes a SHA-256 hex digest of a canonicalized raw workout fragment using the Web Crypto API.

nodes.name: runtime-config.ts
nodes.summary: Reads and exposes runtime configuration values injected at deploy time, specifically the Cloudflare Analytics token which is replaced in the HTML by a build-time placeholder substitution mechanism.

nodes.name: scrub-analytics-string.ts
nodes.summary: Provides scrubAnalyticsString, which sanitizes user-generated strings for analytics event payloads by replacing PII patterns (emails, URLs, digits) with placeholders and truncating to a safe length.

nodes.name: scrubAnalyticsString
nodes.summary: Replaces PII patterns (emails, URLs, numeric sequences) in a string with safe placeholders and enforces a maximum length for analytics use.

nodes.name: secure-storage.ts
nodes.summary: Factory createSecureStorage that wraps localStorage with AES encryption/decryption via the project crypto module, providing a typed key-value store with set/get/remove/clear operations for sensitive data.

nodes.name: createSecureStorage
nodes.summary: Creates a secure localStorage wrapper with passphrase-based AES encryption for get/set/remove/clear operations.

nodes.name: zod-v3-stub.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a minimal stub re-exporting ZodFirstPartyTypeKind and related type constants to bridge compatibility between Zod v3 and v4 APIs used within the SPA.

nodes.name: zone-method-types.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the shared TypeScript types ZoneMethod and ZoneMethodDefault that describe zone calculation method descriptors used across HR, pace, and power method registries.

nodes.name: zone-methods.ts
nodes.summary: Aggregates HR, pace, and power zone methods into a unified facade, re-exporting all method arrays and providing getMethodsForType, findMethod, and getDefaultMethodId helpers consumed by the workout editor UI.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: React SPA entry point that bootstraps the application by wiring providers (persistence, coaching registry, analytics), mounting the root App component into the DOM, and configuring the router base.

nodes.summary: AI agent guidance for the ports directory, documenting the hexagonal-architecture port contracts (PersistencePort and sub-repositories), working conventions, testing requirements, known implementations, and dependencies.

nodes.name: auto-match-dismissal-repository.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the AutoMatchDismissalRepository port interface for persisting user dismissals of auto-match suggestions in the coaching sync feature.

nodes.name: coaching-repositories.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the CoachingRepository and CoachingSyncStateRepository port interfaces for reading and persisting coaching activity records and their sync states.

nodes.summary: Barrel re-export of the PersistencePort and all sub-repository types from the ports module, providing the public API surface for the hexagonal persistence layer.

nodes.name: PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive project structure guide covering folder layout, import conventions (direct imports vs barrel exports), path aliases, atomic design principles, file naming conventions, and the rationale for avoiding barrel re-exports.

nodes.name: router-base.ts
nodes.summary: Single-function utility that computes the React Router base path by stripping the trailing filename segment from the app's base URL.

nodes.summary: AI agent guidance for the routing directory, documenting the purpose of routing constants and helper files and their internal dependencies.

nodes.name: constants.ts
nodes.summary: Exports ROUTE_HEADING_ATTR and ROUTE_HEADING_SELECTOR constants used to mark and query DOM headings for route-based scroll-into-view behaviour in the SPA.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: AI agent guidance for the store/actions/_helpers directory, documenting its role providing shared utilities for Zustand store action implementations and their internal dependencies.

nodes.name: build-krd-with-workout.ts
nodes.summary: Helper that reconstructs a KRD object with a given workout payload, used by action functions to produce the next KRD state after mutations.

nodes.name: buildKrdWithWorkout
nodes.summary: Returns a new KRD object with the workout field replaced by the supplied workout, preserving all other KRD fields.

nodes.name: extract-workout.ts
nodes.summary: Helper that extracts and type-narrows the structured workout from a KRD, throwing if the workout is absent or malformed.

nodes.name: extractStructuredWorkout
nodes.summary: Extracts and validates the workout from a KRD, asserting that it is a structured workout type.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting all action helper utilities (buildKrdWithWorkout, extractStructuredWorkout, replaceBlockAtPosition) from the _helpers subdirectory.

nodes.name: replace-block-at-position.ts
nodes.summary: Helper that immutably replaces a repetition block at a given position in the workout's steps array.

nodes.name: replaceBlockAtPosition
nodes.summary: Returns a new workout with the step at the specified position replaced by the given block.

nodes.name: add-step-to-repetition-block-action.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store action that adds a new workout step into an existing repetition block, updating focus state and dispatching a KRD update.

nodes.name: addStepToRepetitionBlockAction
nodes.summary: Locates a repetition block by ID, appends a new default step to it, rebuilds the KRD, and triggers a workout update action with focus on the created item.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: AI agent guidance document for the store/actions directory, cataloguing step actions, block actions, cross-cutting helpers, testing requirements, and common patterns for working in this area.

nodes.name: clear-expired-deletes-action.test-fixtures.ts
nodes.summary: Test fixture constants for the clear-expired-deletes action tests, providing time offsets, step indices, duration values, watt levels, and history index sentinels.

nodes.name: clear-expired-deletes-action.ts
nodes.summary: Store action that removes soft-deleted steps whose deletion timestamp has exceeded the expiry window, pruning the pending-delete queue.

nodes.name: clearExpiredDeletesAction
nodes.summary: Filters the store's deletedSteps array to only keep entries newer than the expiry threshold, mutating state in place.

nodes.name: copy-paste-integration.test-fixtures.ts
nodes.summary: Integration test fixture constants for copy-paste scenarios, covering step indices, expected step counts after paste, duration values, watt levels, and pre-built clipboard payloads.

nodes.name: copy-step-action.ts
nodes.summary: Store action that serializes a workout step or repetition block at the given index to the clipboard store for later paste.

nodes.name: copyStepAction
nodes.summary: Finds the step at the given index (or repetition block), validates nesting constraints, serializes it, and writes to the clipboard store.

nodes.name: create-empty-repetition-block-action.ts
nodes.summary: Store action that inserts a new empty repetition block (with a single placeholder step) at the end of the workout, recalculating step indices and setting focus.

nodes.name: createEmptyRepetitionBlockAction
nodes.summary: Creates a repetition block with a single empty step, assigns IDs, appends it to the workout, recalculates indices, and dispatches a KRD update with focus.

nodes.name: create-repetition-block-action.test-fixtures.ts
nodes.summary: Test fixture constants for the create-repetition-block action, including iteration counts, step counts, and property-test configuration values.

nodes.name: create-repetition-block-action.ts
nodes.summary: Store action that groups selected workout steps into a new repetition block, removing them from the flat list and inserting the block in their place.

nodes.name: createRepetitionBlockAction
nodes.summary: Extracts selected steps, wraps them in a new repetition block at the computed insert position, re-indexes remaining steps, and dispatches a KRD update.

nodes.name: create-step-action.ts
nodes.summary: Store action that appends a new default workout step to the end of the workout and sets focus to the newly created step.

nodes.name: createStepAction
nodes.summary: Builds a new workout step with default values and a generated ID, appends it to the workout, and dispatches a KRD update with focus on the new step.

nodes.name: delete-repetition-block-action.ts
nodes.summary: Store action that removes a repetition block from the workout by ID, inlines its constituent steps with re-indexed positions, and schedules a soft-delete for undo support.

nodes.name: deleteRepetitionBlockAction
nodes.summary: Finds a repetition block, expands it back to individual steps, removes the block, records a timestamped soft-delete entry, and dispatches a KRD update with adjusted focus.

nodes.name: delete-step-action.ts
nodes.summary: Store action that removes a workout step at the given index, records a timestamped soft-delete, and advances focus to the next adjacent step.

nodes.name: deleteStepAction
nodes.summary: Finds the step to delete, filters it out, re-indexes remaining steps, records a soft-delete with timestamp, computes the next focus target, and dispatches a KRD update.

nodes.name: delete-step-helpers.ts
nodes.summary: Pure helper functions for the delete-step action: locating the target step, filtering it from the list, and re-indexing the remaining steps.

nodes.name: findStepToDelete
nodes.summary: Finds and returns the workout step at the specified step index, distinguishing flat steps from block-nested steps.

nodes.name: filterSteps
nodes.summary: Returns a new steps array with the step at the given index removed.

nodes.name: reindexSteps
nodes.summary: Re-assigns sequential step_index values to a steps array after a deletion.

nodes.name: duplicate-step-action.ts
nodes.summary: Store action that deep-clones a workout step at the given index, assigns a new ID, inserts the clone after the original, and sets focus to the duplicate.

nodes.name: duplicateStepAction
nodes.summary: Deep-clones the step via JSON round-trip, assigns a fresh ID, splices it after its source, re-indexes all steps, rebuilds the KRD, and dispatches an update with focus on the clone.

nodes.name: duplicate-step-in-repetition-block-action.ts
nodes.summary: Store action that deep-clones a step inside a repetition block, assigns a new ID, inserts it after the original in the block, and sets focus to the duplicate.

nodes.name: duplicateStepInRepetitionBlockAction
nodes.summary: Finds the block by ID, structuredClone-duplicates the target step with a new ID, splices it after the original, re-indexes the block's steps, and dispatches a KRD update with focus.

nodes.name: edit-repetition-block-action.ts
nodes.summary: Store action that updates the repeat count of an existing repetition block and dispatches a KRD update.

nodes.name: editRepetitionBlockAction
nodes.summary: Extracts the workout, locates the block by ID, sets the new repeat count, replaces the block in the steps array, rebuilds the KRD, and dispatches a workout update.

nodes.name: error-recovery-actions.ts
nodes.summary: Store actions for error recovery: creating a deep-clone backup of the current state, restoring from that backup, and toggling safe mode on or off.

nodes.name: createBackupAction
nodes.summary: Creates a deep clone of the current workout store state and stores it as a backup snapshot.

nodes.name: restoreFromBackupAction
nodes.summary: Restores the workout store state from the previously saved backup snapshot.

nodes.name: history-actions.ts
nodes.summary: Store actions implementing undo and redo for the workout editor, including focus restoration logic that navigates to the appropriate step after a history jump.

nodes.name: createUndoAction
nodes.summary: Moves the history pointer back one step, restores the previous KRD snapshot, and applies undo-specific focus recovery.

nodes.name: createRedoAction
nodes.summary: Advances the history pointer forward one step, restores the next KRD snapshot, and restores focus to the relevant step.

nodes.name: paste-step-action.ts
nodes.summary: Store action that reads the clipboard, validates the payload as a workout step or repetition block, regenerates IDs, inserts it at the target index, and returns an updated KRD.

nodes.name: pasteStepAction
nodes.summary: Reads the clipboard text, parses it as a workout step or block, regenerates all IDs to avoid duplicates, inserts at the requested position, and returns the new KRD with a success message.

nodes.name: paste-step-helpers.ts
nodes.summary: Pure helper functions for the paste-step action: ID regeneration, clipboard reading, step insertion with recalculated indices, KRD reconstruction, and success message generation.

nodes.name: regeneratePasteIds
nodes.summary: Assigns fresh IDs to all steps in the pasted payload (including nested block steps) to prevent ID collisions.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive inventory of all repo-wide scripts in the scripts/ directory, covering active CI scripts, manual maintainer tools, subdirectory descriptions, and authoring guidelines for new scripts.

nodes.name: sync-extension-version.mjs

nodes.name: sync-popup-css.mjs
nodes.summary: Short utility script that copies or synchronizes popup CSS from the SPA build output into the Chrome extension package, ensuring consistent styling between the SPA editor and the extension popup.

nodes.name: validate-package.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script that validates a built npm package by checking that its dist artifacts exist, package.json fields are correctly set, and the package can be consumed without import errors.

nodes.languageNotes: Acts as the base tsconfig that all package-level configs extend via the 'extends' field, centralizing strict TypeScript settings for the entire monorepo.

layers.name: Domain & Core
layers.description: Pure domain types, Zod schemas, port interfaces, and the logger adapter in @kaiord/core — the dependency-free foundation that all other packages build on.

layers.name: Application & Use Cases
layers.description: Core use-case orchestration in @kaiord/core/application and the SPA-side application layer (AI workout processor, Dexie persistence use-cases, profile management) that coordinate domain logic without depending on adapters.

layers.name: Format Adapters
layers.description: Concrete readers, writers, and validators for FIT, TCX, ZWO, and Garmin GCN formats — the hexagonal adapter packages that translate between external file formats and the canonical KRD representation.

layers.name: External Integrations
layers.description: Garmin Connect API client (SSO auth, workout push/list) and AI provider adapters (LLM text-to-workout conversion) — packages that integrate with third-party remote services.

layers.name: Interfaces & Entry Points
layers.description: The CLI command-line interface (@kaiord/cli) and MCP Model Context Protocol server (@kaiord/mcp) — the surface layer through which users and AI agents interact with the kaiord framework.

layers.name: Web Editor UI
layers.description: React components, pages, and layout of the workout-spa-editor — the visual workout builder built with atomic design, Tailwind CSS, and Vite.

layers.name: Web Editor State & Logic
layers.description: Zustand store (undo/redo, clipboard, selection), React hooks, Dexie persistence adapters, contexts, ports, types, and utilities that drive the workout-spa-editor's runtime behaviour independently of the rendered UI.

layers.name: Browser Extensions
layers.description: The garmin-bridge and train2go-bridge Chrome extensions that inject content scripts into Garmin Connect and Train2Go to push and read workouts from the browser.

layers.name: Sites & Marketing
layers.description: The kaiord landing page, VitePress documentation site, and root-level brand assets/styles — the public-facing web presence separate from the application.

layers.name: Tooling & Build
layers.description: Root and per-package build configuration (tsconfig, vite, vitest, eslint, tsup, changeset), repo scripts, git hooks, test fixtures, .claude agents, and generated tooling artefacts.

layers.name: CI/CD Pipelines
layers.description: GitHub Actions workflow files covering CI, release, changeset bot, CWS publishing, E2E tests, security scans, accessibility baselines, and metrics gating.

layers.name: Documentation & Specs
layers.description: Project documentation, AGENTS.md guidance files, OpenSpec feature proposals, domain specs, archived change logs, and README/CONTRIBUTING/CHANGELOG markdown across all packages.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: The README is the best starting point for understanding what Kaiord is and why it exists. It describes a TypeScript monorepo that creates, converts, and manages health and fitness data across five file formats — FIT, TCX, ZWO, GCN, and the project's own canonical KRD format — plus a CLI, an MCP server, and a visual workout editor. Reading it first gives you the vocabulary (KRD, adapters, ports) and the mental map of the 11 packages you are about to explore.

tour.title: Core Package Entry Point
tour.description: The @kaiord/core barrel (`packages/core/src/index.ts`) is the single public surface of the entire framework. It re-exports domain types, Zod schemas, port interfaces, application use-cases, the logger adapter, round-trip test utilities, and profile-snapshot types — everything any adapter or consumer needs, and nothing more. Understanding what this file exports tells you what the framework considers its stable API contract.

tour.title: KRD Domain Model
tour.description: The domain layer (`packages/core/src/domain/index.ts`) contains only pure types and Zod schemas — zero external dependencies. The KRD schema (`packages/core/src/domain/schemas/krd.ts`) is the canonical representation that every adapter must ultimately produce or consume; the workout schema (`packages/core/src/domain/schemas/workout.ts`) composes sport, sub-sport, and step sub-schemas into the top-level structure every format converter targets. This is the shared vocabulary that makes the entire format-agnostic pipeline possible.
tour.languageLesson: Zod schemas serve double duty here: they validate at runtime AND infer TypeScript types via `z.infer<typeof krdSchema>`, eliminating the need to maintain separate interface definitions. This pattern appears throughout the codebase — one schema file is both the validator and the type source.

tour.title: Port Interfaces (Strategy Pattern)
tour.description: The ports directory (`packages/core/src/ports/index.ts`) defines the contracts that decouple the core from every concrete adapter. `format-strategy.ts` declares `BinaryReader`, `BinaryWriter`, `TextReader`, and `TextWriter` — the four slot types into which any format adapter plugs. `workout-service.ts` defines the `WorkoutService` port for remote operations like push and list. Nothing in core imports an adapter; the dependency always flows inward.
tour.languageLesson: This is the hexagonal (ports and adapters) architecture in its purest form. Ports are TypeScript interfaces defined in the core; adapters are classes or objects in separate packages that implement those interfaces. The core never `import`s an adapter — it only accepts one as a function argument.

tour.title: Application Use-Cases
tour.description: The application layer (`packages/core/src/application/index.ts`) exposes exactly four functions: `fromBinary`, `fromText`, `toBinary`, and `toText`. Each function in `from-format.ts` / `to-format.ts` accepts an injected reader or writer strategy, validates the KRD with Zod, and delegates all format-specific work to the injected adapter. These four functions are the entire public API for format conversion — callers wire in the adapter, and the core orchestrates the rest.
tour.languageLesson: By accepting a reader/writer as a plain function argument rather than through a class constructor, these use-cases stay stateless and trivially testable — you can pass a mock reader in a unit test and never touch the filesystem.

tour.title: Format Adapters: FIT, ZWO & GCN
tour.description: Each adapter package exposes pre-built singletons and factory functions that satisfy the port interfaces from Step 4. `packages/fit/src/index.ts` provides `fitReader`/`fitWriter` backed by the Garmin FIT SDK. `packages/zwo/src/index.ts` provides ZWO XML reader/writer with XSD validation; `packages/zwo/src/adapters/krd-to-zwift.converter.ts` shows how a KRD object is orchestrated into a ZWO XML string. `packages/garmin/src/index.ts` exposes the Garmin GCN JSON adapter. These three packages demonstrate the full strategy pattern: each plugs into `fromText`/`toText` from Step 5 without any modification to core.

tour.title: Garmin Connect Integration
tour.description: The `@kaiord/garmin-connect` package (`packages/garmin-connect/src/index.ts`) implements the `WorkoutService` port from Step 4 against the real Garmin Connect API. `garmin-connect-client.ts` wires together auth, token manager, HTTP client, and retry logic into one factory. `garmin-auth-provider.ts` orchestrates the full SSO login flow (OAuth1 consumer → login ticket → OAuth2 token exchange) described in `garmin-sso.ts`. This is the most complex external integration in the codebase and shows how the port abstraction isolates network-level concerns from the rest of the framework.

tour.title: CLI Entry Point
tour.description: The CLI package entry point (`packages/cli/src/bin/kaiord.ts`) bootstraps yargs, registers all commands, attaches global flags (--verbose, --json, --log-format), and hands off to the command tree. The convert command (`packages/cli/src/commands/convert/index.ts`) is the flagship: it resolves config, validates options, creates a logger, and dispatches to batch or single-file conversion flows — each delegation calling the `fromText`/`toBinary` use-cases from Step 5 with the appropriate adapter injected. This shows how the framework's core API surfaces to end users.

tour.title: MCP Server
tour.description: The `@kaiord/mcp` package (`packages/mcp/src/index.ts`) exposes the same framework capabilities to AI/LLM agents through the Model Context Protocol. `create-server.ts` is the most fan-out-heavy file in the repo: it registers all tools, resources, and prompts in a single factory, wiring in the same adapters used by the CLI. Understanding this file shows how the framework's hexagonal design lets a completely different interface (JSON-RPC tool calls from an LLM) reuse the same KRD conversion pipeline with no changes to core.
tour.languageLesson: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an open standard for giving LLMs structured access to tools and resources. The server registers each operation (e.g. convert-fit-to-krd) as a named tool with a JSON Schema input spec — the LLM calls it like a function and receives structured output.

tour.title: React SPA Entry & Root Component
tour.description: The workout editor is a full React SPA. `main.tsx` is the bootstrap: it wires up providers (persistence context, coaching registry, analytics) and mounts the root. `App.tsx` is the root component that initialises store hydration, profile sync, onboarding, and the router. These two files together reveal the SPA's provider hierarchy — and they show that the SPA is architecturally independent from the CLI and MCP packages, reusing only the KRD types from @kaiord/core.

tour.title: SPA State: Zustand, Dexie & Ports
tour.description: The SPA's state layer is split across three concerns. The persistence port (`packages/workout-spa-editor/src/ports/persistence-port.ts`) is the SPA-side equivalent of the core ports: a `PersistencePort` interface aggregating all repository sub-interfaces. `dexie-database.ts` defines the `KaiordDatabase` Dexie subclass with all versioned migrations; `dexie-persistence-adapter.ts` composes all individual Dexie repositories into a single adapter. The Zustand store (`workout-store.ts`) is separate and handles only editor runtime state — undo/redo, selection, clipboard — never touching Dexie directly. This three-way split enforces the rule: editor runtime → Zustand, persisted data → Dexie.
tour.languageLesson: Dexie's `useLiveQuery` hook subscribes React components directly to IndexedDB queries — when a workout is saved, every component that queries it re-renders automatically, with no manual cache invalidation. Zustand's `create` with slice composition keeps the editor store modular without a top-level god object.

tour.title: AI Generation & Browser Bridges
tour.description: Two advanced integration points round out the architecture. `useAiGeneration.ts` is the SPA hook that drives text-to-workout AI generation: it resolves the configured LLM provider, assembles a prompt with profile data, calls the AI endpoint, and parses the response back into a KRD. The browser bridge content scripts (`packages/garmin-bridge/content.js` and `packages/train2go-bridge/content.js`) complete the loop: Chrome extensions injected into Garmin Connect and Train2Go intercept native fetch requests so the SPA can push/pull workouts and read coaching plans directly from those third-party sites without a backend server.
